summary: Manage TLS certificates and their load-balancer bindings: add or remove certificate tags, request a new DNS-validated certificate, attach an extra SNI certificate to a listener, swap a listener's default certificate (engineer-only — it changes what live clients are served), and delete a confirmed-unused certificate. DNS validation records are managed outside this catalog.

operations.title: Add a tag to a certificate
operations.summary: Add a metadata tag to a certificate. Additive and reversible, with no effect on validation or the listeners using it.
operations.description: Add one metadata tag key/value to an ACM certificate. Additive and reversible; no impact on validation state or on listeners consuming the certificate.

params.name: certificate
params.label: Certificate
params.help: The existing ACM certificate to tag, chosen from the estate inventory so only a real, Terraform-managed certificate can be targeted; adding a tag is metadata-only and has no effect on validation state or the listeners serving the certificate.

params.name: tag_key
params.label: Tag key
params.help: Letters/digits/_/- , 2-65 chars. Governance keys are engineer-owned.

params.name: tag_value
params.label: Tag value
params.help: Free-text value stored under the tag key, 1-128 characters; tag values are pure metadata on the certificate, so any text within the length bound is safe and never affects TLS behavior.

operations.title: Remove a tag from a certificate
operations.summary: Remove a metadata tag from a certificate. Reversible - protected governance tags can't be removed this way.
operations.description: Delete one metadata tag from an ACM certificate. Reversible - the tag can be re-added. Governance tags such as Environment or CostCentre are protected and rejected.

params.name: certificate
params.label: Certificate
params.help: The existing ACM certificate to remove the tag from, chosen from the estate inventory so only a real, Terraform-managed certificate can be targeted; the change is metadata-only and reversible by re-adding the tag.

params.name: tag_key
params.label: Tag key to remove
params.help: The tag key to delete. Governance keys (Environment, CostCentre) are engineer-owned and rejected.

operations.title: Request a new ACM-managed certificate (DNS-validated)
operations.summary: Request a new certificate for one or more domains. Publish the DNS records to validate it; it then renews itself automatically.
operations.description: Issue a brand-new Amazon-managed certificate for one or more hostnames, validated via DNS CNAME records. Unlike the estate's imported wildcard certificate, an ACM-managed certificate auto-renews - but the DNS validation records must be published by whoever controls the zone before it reaches ISSUED.

params.name: primary_domain_name
params.label: Primary domain (FQDN)
params.help: e.g. app.example.com or *.example.com (wildcard only as leftmost label).

params.name: validation_method
params.label: Validation method
params.help: DNS only - EMAIL validation requires manual approver clicks and is rejected by the catalog.

params.name: subject_alternative_names
params.label: Additional hostnames (SANs)

bounds.semantic: each an FQDN the org controls; total SANs incl. primary <= 100

params.help: Optional extra hostnames covered by the same certificate.

params.name: tags
params.label: Tags
params.help: Optional resource tags.

operations.title: Attach an additional certificate to a listener (SNI)
operations.summary: Attach an extra certificate to a listener for other hostnames. Additive - the listener's default certificate is untouched.
operations.description: Associate an already-ISSUED certificate with an HTTPS listener as an extra SNI certificate, alongside its default certificate. Additive and reversible - the listener's default certificate is untouched and existing connections are unaffected.

params.name: listener
params.label: Listener
params.help: The existing ALB HTTPS listener that will serve the extra SNI certificate, chosen from the estate inventory so only a real, Terraform-managed listener can be targeted; its default certificate and existing connections are untouched by this additive change.

params.name: certificate
params.label: Certificate to attach
params.help: Must already be ISSUED and in the same region as the listener.

operations.title: Swap the default certificate on a listener
operations.summary: Point a listener at a new certificate to replace one that's expiring. A mismatch breaks TLS for every client, so an engineer reviews it.
operations.description: Re-point a listener's default certificate_arn to a different ISSUED certificate - the standard remediation for a certificate nearing expiry. Updates in place, but a mismatched or not-yet-issued certificate causes an immediate TLS failure for every client on that listener.

params.name: listener
params.label: Listener
params.help: The existing ALB HTTPS listener whose default certificate_arn is being re-pointed, chosen from the estate inventory so only a real, Terraform-managed listener can be targeted; every client connecting to this listener is affected by the swap, which is why an engineer authors and reviews it.

params.name: new_certificate
params.label: New default certificate
params.help: Must be ISSUED, same region as the listener, and cover the hostnames it serves.

operations.title: Delete an unused certificate
operations.summary: Permanently delete a certificate confirmed to be unused. This can't be undone, and protected certificates can't be selected.
operations.description: Permanently destroy an ACM certificate confirmed unattached to any listener, CloudFront distribution, or API Gateway. Irreversible - a destroyed certificate cannot be recreated and would need re-issue/re-import plus re-validation. Certificates with lifecycle prevent_destroy set (e.g. the estate's imported wildcard) cannot be targeted.

params.name: certificate
params.label: Certificate
params.help: The ACM certificate to permanently destroy, chosen from the estate inventory so only a real, Terraform-managed certificate can be targeted; deletion is irreversible, and certificates guarded by lifecycle prevent_destroy (such as the estate's imported wildcard) cannot be selected.

params.name: confirm_unused
params.label: Confirm unused
params.help: Typed confirmation that no listener, CloudFront distribution, or API Gateway references this certificate's ARN.

operations.title: Toggle certificate transparency logging
operations.summary: Turn certificate transparency logging on or off. Turning it off only stops future logging - past entries stay public.
operations.description: Set options.certificate_transparency_logging_preference to ENABLED or DISABLED. Updates in place per the AWS provider — no ForceNew, no downtime, fully reversible (re-enabling always works; note AWS cannot remove an entry already written to the public CT log).

params.name: certificate
params.label: Certificate
params.help: Which ACM certificate to update.

params.name: preference
params.label: Transparency logging
params.help: ENABLED publishes the cert to public Certificate Transparency logs; DISABLED opts out of future logging.

operations.title: Set early renewal duration
operations.summary: Set how many days before expiry ACM starts renewing this certificate automatically.
operations.description: Set early_renewal_duration (ISO 8601 period, e.g. P90D) controlling how far before expiry ACM initiates managed renewal. Updates in place — no ForceNew, no downtime, reversible. Bounded to common renewal windows.

params.name: certificate
params.label: Certificate
params.help: Which ACM certificate to update.

params.name: duration
params.label: Early renewal window
params.help: ISO 8601 period before expiry at which ACM renews the certificate.

operations.title: Set certificate private-key export
operations.summary: Allow or block exporting this certificate's private key. Enabling export can't be safely undone once used, so an engineer reviews it.
operations.description: Set options.export (ENABLED/DISABLED), which governs whether the certificate's private key can be exported from ACM. Security exposure: enabling export lets private key material leave ACM and cannot be safely undone once used. Bounded enum but engineer-reviewed given the blast radius and provider caveats around exportable certificates.

params.name: certificate
params.label: Certificate
params.help: Which ACM certificate to update.

params.name: export
params.label: Key export
params.help: ENABLED makes the private key exportable from ACM — treat as irreversible security exposure.

operations.title: Request another change to ACM certificate
operations.summary: Request any other change to a certificate, including ones that replace it. An engineer authors and reviews the Terraform.
operations.description: Catch-all for any change to an ACM certificate not offered as a bounded self-service form — including ForceNew edits (domain_name, subject_alternative_names, validation_method, key_algorithm, certificate_authority_arn, imported key/body/chain). An engineer authors and reviews the Terraform.

params.name: certificate
params.label: Certificate
params.help: The certificate to change, chosen from live inventory.

params.name: purpose
params.label: What change do you need?
params.help: Anything not offered as a bounded form — an engineer authors the Terraform.

operations.title: Set the listener's TLS security policy
operations.summary: Set which TLS versions and ciphers this listener accepts. A stricter policy can lock out very old clients; it applies with no downtime.
operations.description: Updates ssl_policy on an HTTPS listener — the named policy that fixes which TLS versions and cipher suites clients may negotiate. In-place, reversible, no downtime; a stricter policy can lock out very old clients, so it is guardrailed.

params.name: listener
params.label: Listener
params.help: The HTTPS listener whose TLS policy changes, picked from the estate's live listeners. Plain HTTP listeners carry no TLS policy.

params.name: tls_policy
params.label: TLS security policy
params.help: Which TLS versions and ciphers clients may use. The 2021 TLS 1.3 policy is the AWS-recommended default.

operations.title: Set listener protocol
operations.summary: Change the listener's protocol, for example HTTPS to HTTP. This can silently drop encryption for every client, so an engineer reviews it.
operations.description: Set protocol for client-to-load-balancer connections. HIGH: can silently remove transport encryption (e.g. HTTPS->HTTP) and breaks every client expecting the prior protocol, so engineer-reviewed. Allowlist restricted to the provider-documented ALB/NLB protocols.

params.name: listener
params.label: Listener
params.help: The listener to change, chosen from live inventory.

params.name: protocol
params.label: Protocol
params.help: ALB: HTTP/HTTPS; NLB: TCP/TLS/UDP/TCP_UDP/QUIC/TCP_QUIC.

operations.title: Toggle HTTP response Server header
operations.summary: Show or hide the Server header in this listener's HTTP responses. A minor detail with no effect on routing or encryption.
operations.description: Set routing_http_response_server_enabled (ALB HTTP/HTTPS) to add or remove the response Server header. Updates in place, reversible, no downtime; a minor info-disclosure toggle. Bounded to the fixed true/false.

params.name: listener
params.label: Listener
params.help: The listener to change, chosen from live inventory.

params.name: enabled
params.label: Server header enabled
params.help: true keeps the response Server header; false removes it.

operations.title: Set CORS Access-Control-Allow-Methods header
operations.summary: Set which HTTP methods this listener allows for cross-origin (CORS) requests.
operations.description: Set routing_http_response_access_control_allow_methods_header_value (ALB) — the HTTP methods ALB advertises as allowed cross-origin. Updates in place, reversible. Each selected method is bounded to the provider's closed set of eight methods.

params.name: listener
params.label: Listener
params.help: The listener to change, chosen from live inventory.

params.name: methods
params.label: Allowed methods
params.help: Each entry must be one of the eight provider-documented methods; joined into the header value.

operations.title: Set CORS Access-Control-Allow-Credentials header
operations.summary: Allow credentialed cross-origin requests here. Paired with a permissive origin this is a serious security hole, so an engineer reviews it.
operations.description: Set routing_http_response_access_control_allow_credentials_header_value. HIGH: enabling credentialed cross-origin requests combined with a permissive allow-origin is a classic high-severity CORS vulnerability, so engineer-reviewed. The provider's only valid value is true; removing the header disables it.

params.name: listener
params.label: Listener
params.help: The listener to change, chosen from live inventory.

params.name: value
params.label: Allow-credentials
params.help: Provider's only valid value is true.

operations.title: Add a tag to a listener
operations.summary: Add a metadata tag to a listener. No effect on routing or TLS.
operations.description: Add one metadata tag key/value to an aws_lb_listener. Additive, reversible, no impact on routing or TLS termination. Key and value are pattern-bounded, mirroring the certificate tag operation.

params.name: listener
params.label: Listener
params.help: The listener to change, chosen from live inventory.

params.name: tag_key
params.label: Tag key
params.help: Letters/digits/_/- , 2-65 chars.

params.name: tag_value
params.label: Tag value
params.help: The tag value (1–128 characters).

operations.title: Set default action evaluation order
operations.summary: Change the order this listener's default actions run in. Reordering can bypass an authentication step, so an engineer reviews it.
operations.description: Set default_action.order — the evaluation sequence of a default action. HIGH: reordering can place a forward action ahead of an authenticate action and bypass the auth boundary, so engineer-reviewed. Bounded to the provider range 1-50000.

params.name: listener
params.label: Listener
params.help: The listener to change, chosen from live inventory.

operations.title: Set how Cognito treats unauthenticated requests
operations.summary: Choose how this listener's Cognito login handles a request with no session: deny, allow through, or prompt to log in. Engineer-reviewed.
operations.description: Set default_action.authenticate_cognito.on_unauthenticated_request — whether unauthenticated requests are denied, allowed, or challenged. HIGH: this is the access-control decision itself, so engineer-reviewed. Allowlist per provider docs.

params.name: listener
params.label: Listener
params.help: The listener to change, chosen from live inventory.

params.name: behavior
params.label: On unauthenticated request
params.help: How to handle an unauthenticated request: deny blocks it, allow lets it through unauthenticated, authenticate challenges the user to log in.

operations.title: Set how OIDC treats unauthenticated requests
operations.summary: Choose how this listener's OIDC login handles a request with no session: deny, allow through, or prompt to log in. Engineer-reviewed.
operations.description: Set default_action.authenticate_oidc.on_unauthenticated_request — whether unauthenticated requests are denied, allowed, or challenged. HIGH: this is the access-control decision itself, so engineer-reviewed. Allowlist per provider docs.

params.name: listener
params.label: Listener
params.help: The listener to change, chosen from live inventory.

params.name: behavior
params.label: On unauthenticated request
params.help: How to handle an unauthenticated request: deny blocks it, allow lets it through unauthenticated, authenticate challenges the user to log in.

operations.title: Set fixed-response content type
operations.summary: Set the content type of this listener's fixed static response, such as plain text or JSON.
operations.description: Set default_action.fixed_response.content_type. Updates in place, reversible, no downtime; the cosmetic MIME type of a static response. Allowlist per provider docs.

params.name: listener
params.label: Listener
params.help: The listener to change, chosen from live inventory.

params.name: content_type
params.label: Content type
params.help: MIME type of the fixed response body returned to the client.

operations.title: Set fixed-response status code
operations.summary: Set the HTTP status code this listener's fixed response returns. If that response is active, every client hitting it sees the new code.
operations.description: Set default_action.fixed_response.status_code. Updates in place, reversible. If this is the active action it changes the HTTP status every client receives. Bounded by pattern to the provider's 2XX/4XX/5XX classes.

params.name: listener
params.label: Listener
params.help: The listener to change, chosen from live inventory.

params.name: status_code
params.label: Status code
params.help: A 2xx, 4xx, or 5xx code, e.g. 200, 404, 503.

operations.title: Set forward target-group weight
operations.summary: Adjust how much traffic this listener sends to one target group versus the others. A skewed weight can overload or starve a group.
operations.description: Set default_action.forward.target_group.weight for one target group in the listener's forward action — the traffic-split weighting. Updates in place, reversible; can overload or starve a target group so guardrailed. Bounded to the provider range 0-999.

params.name: listener
params.label: Listener
params.help: The listener to change, chosen from live inventory.

params.name: target_group
params.label: Target group
params.help: Which target group in the forward action to reweight.

operations.title: Configure forward stickiness
operations.summary: Turn session stickiness on or off for this listener and set how long it lasts, keeping repeat requests on the same target.
operations.description: Configure default_action.forward.stickiness — enable/disable target-group session affinity and set its duration together (setting a duration is meaningless without the enabled flag). Updates in place, reversible, single-listener scope. enabled bounded to true/false; duration bounded to the provider range 1-604800 seconds (7 days).

params.name: listener
params.label: Listener
params.help: The listener to change, chosen from live inventory.

params.name: enabled
params.label: Stickiness enabled
params.help: Configure forward stickiness: true enables configure forward stickiness, false disables it.

params.name: duration
params.label: Duration (seconds)
params.help: 1-604800 seconds; applies when enabled is true.

operations.title: Set redirect status code
operations.summary: Choose permanent or temporary for this listener's redirect. A permanent one can stay cached in browsers even after you change it back.
operations.description: Set default_action.redirect.status_code — HTTP_301 (permanent) or HTTP_302 (temporary). Updates in place, reversible; note a 301 may be cached client-side and can appear sticky even after reverting. Allowlist per provider docs.

params.name: listener
params.label: Listener
params.help: The listener to change, chosen from live inventory.

params.name: status_code
params.label: Redirect status code
params.help: Redirect type: HTTP_301 is a permanent redirect, HTTP_302 a temporary one.

operations.title: Set redirect target port
operations.summary: Set which port this listener's redirect sends clients to.
operations.description: Set default_action.redirect.port — the port clients are redirected to. Updates in place, reversible. Bounded to 1-65535; the #{port} placeholder form is intentionally excluded (use the engineer catch-all if the dynamic placeholder is required).

params.name: listener
params.label: Listener
params.help: The listener to change, chosen from live inventory.

operations.title: Set redirect protocol
operations.summary: Set the protocol this listener redirects to. Switching HTTPS to HTTP can undermine encryption for every client, so an engineer reviews it.
operations.description: Set default_action.redirect.protocol. HIGH: can downgrade an HTTPS-enforcing redirect to HTTP and undermine TLS enforcement for every client hitting this action, so engineer-reviewed. Allowlist HTTP, HTTPS, or the #{protocol} placeholder per provider docs.

params.name: listener
params.label: Listener
params.help: The listener to change, chosen from live inventory.

params.name: protocol
params.label: Redirect protocol
params.help: Protocol to use; #{protocol} preserves the client's incoming protocol.

operations.title: Set mutual authentication mode
operations.summary: Turn this listener's client-certificate check off, pass it through, or verify it. The access decision itself, so an engineer reviews it.
operations.description: Set mutual_authentication.mode — off, passthrough, or verify. HIGH: directly controls whether client-certificate (mTLS) authentication is enforced, the access-control decision itself, so engineer-reviewed. Allowlist per provider docs.

params.name: listener
params.label: Listener
params.help: The listener to change, chosen from live inventory.

params.name: mode
params.label: mTLS mode
params.help: Mutual-TLS mode: off disables mTLS, passthrough forwards the client cert, verify validates it against the trust store.

operations.title: Set mTLS advertise trust-store CA names
operations.summary: Show or hide trusted CA names during this listener's client-certificate handshake. Minor disclosure only, not the access decision.
operations.description: Set mutual_authentication.advertise_trust_store_ca_names — off or on. Updates in place, reversible; controls minor CA-name disclosure during the mTLS handshake, not the access decision. Only valid when mode is verify. Allowlist per provider docs.

params.name: listener
params.label: Listener
params.help: The listener to change, chosen from live inventory.

params.name: value
params.label: Advertise trust-store CA names
params.help: Only valid when mutual_authentication.mode is verify.

operations.title: Set mTLS ignore client-certificate expiry
operations.summary: Let this listener accept expired client certificates. Removes a safety check on that boundary, so an engineer reviews it.
operations.description: Set mutual_authentication.ignore_client_certificate_expiry (true/false). HIGH: enabling it disables a client-certificate expiry check and removes a safety guardrail on the mTLS boundary, so engineer-reviewed. Only valid when mode is verify. Bounded to the fixed true/false.

params.name: listener
params.label: Listener
params.help: The listener to change, chosen from live inventory.

params.name: enabled
params.label: Ignore client-certificate expiry
params.help: Only valid when mutual_authentication.mode is verify.

operations.title: Request another change to this listener
operations.summary: Request any other change to this listener. An engineer authors and reviews the Terraform.
operations.description: Catch-all for any aws_lb_listener change not offered as a bounded self-service form — default-action routing and auth blocks (forward target-group ARNs, authenticate-cognito/oidc, jwt-validation), ssl_policy, load_balancer_arn (ForceNew), region, mutual_authentication trust store, and free-text routing/response headers. An engineer authors and reviews the Terraform. Adds the engineer catch-all targeting aws_lb_listener that acm.json previously lacked, so unbounded/skipped arguments have a reachable engineer path.

params.name: listener
params.label: Listener
params.help: The listener to change, chosen from live inventory.

params.name: purpose
params.label: What change do you need?
params.help: Describe it; an engineer will author the Terraform.

operations.title: Provision a new listener
operations.summary: Request a brand-new HTTP or HTTPS listener on an existing load balancer; an engineer finalizes and reviews the Terraform before it applies.
operations.description: Stand up a new listener as a complete specification: port, protocol, certificate, and a single forwarding rule to a target group. Submitting routes it to an engineer with a draft Terraform block to review and finish — nothing is applied without review.

params.name: listener_name
params.label: Listener name
params.help: Lower-case letters, numbers and dashes. Names the listener in the generated draft — a listener has no name field of its own.

params.name: load_balancer
params.label: Load balancer
params.help: The existing load balancer this listener attaches to.

params.name: port
params.label: Port
params.help: The port this listener accepts connections on, e.g. 443 for HTTPS or 80 for HTTP.

params.name: protocol
params.label: Protocol
params.help: ALB protocol. NLB protocols (TCP/UDP/TLS) are an engineer decision — use the request-a-change catch-all.

params.name: certificate
params.label: Default certificate
params.help: Required for HTTPS. Must already be ISSUED and cover the hostnames this listener serves.

params.name: ssl_policy
params.label: TLS security policy
params.help: Which TLS versions and ciphers clients may use. Required for HTTPS; the 2021 TLS 1.3 policy is the AWS-recommended default.

params.name: default_action_type
params.label: Default action
params.help: Always forward to a target group for the common case — written into the draft, never asked.

params.name: target_group
params.label: Target group
params.help: Where this listener forwards every request by default.

params.name: resource_name
params.label: Listener name tag
params.help: Shown as the name everywhere — consoles, tickets, this portal.

params.name: description_tag
params.label: What this listener serves
params.help: One line an operator can act on at 3 AM.

params.name: pic
params.label: Person in charge
params.help: Who to call about this listener.

decisions: Non-forward default actions (redirect, fixed response, authenticate-cognito/oidc, weighted or multi-target-group forwarding) are engineer decisions — use the request-a-change catch-all or the dedicated set operations after creation
NLB protocols and mutual-TLS (mTLS) trust-store configuration are engineer decisions

operations.title: Provision a new ACM certificate (raw resource)
operations.summary: Author a new ACM certificate as a raw Terraform resource; an engineer finalizes and reviews it, including validation, before it applies.
operations.description: Stand up a new aws_acm_certificate as a complete specification: domain, SANs, validation method and certificate-transparency preference. Submitting routes it to an engineer with a draft Terraform block to review and finish — nothing is applied without review. Prefer the existing guided DNS-validated certificate request for the common case, which also automates the validation records; use this form when the raw resource skeleton is what is needed instead.

params.name: cert_name
params.label: Certificate name
params.help: Lower-case letters, numbers and dashes. Names the certificate in the generated draft — an ACM certificate has no name field of its own.

params.name: domain_name
params.label: Primary domain (FQDN)
params.help: e.g. app.example.com or *.example.com (wildcard only as leftmost label).

params.name: subject_alternative_names
params.label: Additional hostnames (SANs)

bounds.semantic: each an FQDN the org controls; total SANs incl. primary <= 100

params.help: Optional extra hostnames covered by the same certificate.

params.name: validation_method
params.label: Validation method
params.help: DNS only — EMAIL validation requires manual approver clicks and is rejected by the catalog.

params.name: key_algorithm
params.label: Key algorithm
params.help: RSA_2048 is the broadly-compatible default; EC_prime256v1 is smaller/faster if every client supports it.

params.name: certificate_transparency_logging_preference
params.label: Certificate transparency logging
params.help: Public CT logging of the issued certificate. Same setting the existing toggle action changes later.

params.name: resource_name
params.label: Certificate name tag
params.help: Shown as the name everywhere — consoles, tickets, this portal.

params.name: description_tag
params.label: What this certificate is for
params.help: One line an operator can act on at 3 AM.

params.name: pic
params.label: Person in charge
params.help: Who to call about this certificate.

decisions: DNS validation records must be published in the zone before the certificate reaches ISSUED — coordinate with whoever controls the zone, or prefer the existing guided DNS-validated request action, which automates the validation records
Private-CA-issued certificates (certificate_authority_arn) and imported certificates (certificate_body/private_key) are engineer decisions outside this form
